name: Gallecs Rural Area Loop
description: Explore the Espai Rural de Gallecs on an easy 8.1-mile mountain bike loop, featuring rural landscapes and the Santa Maria de Gallecs Church.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Mollet Del Vallès features diverse natural landscapes, including the extensive Gallecs Special Conservation Area, which offers a network of rural trails. The region is characterized by green spaces, agricultural land, and riverside paths along the Besòs River. Terrain varies from family-friendly gravel paths to more challenging routes with significant elevation gains, incorporating both paved and unpaved surfaces. This area serves as a gateway to broader mountain biking opportunities in Catalonia, providing access to varied landscapes from coastal ranges to the Pre-Pyrenees. Best mountain bike trails around Mollet Del Vallès The most popular mountain bike trail is Gallecs Rural Area Loop, an easy 8.1 miles (13.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 9 minutes to complete. This route explores the protected rural landscapes of Gallecs. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Torre Marimon – Sentmenat Castle loop from Mollet-Santa Rosa, a moderate 28.1 miles (45.3 km) path. This route features historical landmarks and varied terrain with notable elevation changes. Local mountain bikers also love the Besòs River Promenade – Cornice Viewpoint loop from Mollet - Sant Fost, a 25.7 miles (41.3 km) trail leading through riverside paths and offering scenic viewpoints, often completed in about 3 hours 31 minutes. Mountain biking around Mollet Del Vallès is defined by its extensive green spaces, the Besòs River paths, and varied terrain that includes both rural tracks and more challenging climbs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to difficult routes with significant elevation. The routes in Mollet Del Vallès are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 290 reviews. More than 2000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Mollet Del Vallès's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of mountain bike trails can I expect around Mollet Del Vallès?
A: Mollet Del Vallès offers a diverse range of mountain biking experiences. You'll find everything from family-friendly gravel paths to more challenging routes with significant elevation gains. The terrain includes extensive green spaces, agricultural land, and riverside paths along the Besòs River, with a mix of paved and unpaved surfaces. The region also serves as a gateway to broader mountain biking opportunities in Catalonia, including coastal ranges and the Pre-Pyrenees.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in the Mollet Del Vallès area?
A: There are over 45 mountain bike trails available around Mollet Del Vallès, catering to various skill levels. This includes 6 easy routes, 26 moderate routes, and 17 difficult routes, ensuring options for every rider.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly mountain bike trails in Mollet Del Vallès?
A: Yes, Mollet Del Vallès offers several easy and family-friendly options. A great example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e956275285/gallecs-rural-area-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gallecs Rural Area Loop&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 8.1-mile (13.1 km) trail that explores the protected rural landscapes of the Gallecs Special Conservation Area. This area is known for its serene natural environment.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39556912" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vallforners Reservoir – Vallforners Reservoir loop from Parets del Vallès&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gains and longer distances. This particular route covers 42 miles (67.6 km) with over 1,100 meters of ascent, providing a demanding ride.

Q: What landmarks or attractions can I see while mountain biking in the area?
A: Many trails pass by interesting landmarks and natural features. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31239559" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre Marimon – Sentmenat Castle loop from Mollet-Santa Rosa&lt;/a&gt; features historical landmarks. You can also explore highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5122420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Besòs Riverside Path: Montcada to Can Zam&lt;/a&gt;, the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1763023" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen of the Roca d'en Toni&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3174771" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Coscollada Observation Tower&lt;/a&gt; for scenic views.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Mollet Del Vallès?
A: The mountain biking routes in Mollet Del Vallès are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 290 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the extensive green spaces, and the well-maintained paths, particularly within the Gallecs Special Conservation Area and along the Besòs River.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that follow rivers or offer water views?
A: Yes, the region features excellent riverside paths. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39556633" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Besòs River Promenade – Cornice Viewpoint loop from Mollet - Sant Fost&lt;/a&gt; is a popular choice, leading through riverside paths and offering scenic viewpoints along the Besòs riverbed. Sections of this path range from paved bike lanes to dirt paths.

Q: Is it possible to find circular mountain bike routes in Mollet Del Vallès?
A: Many of the mountain bike trails in Mollet Del Vallès are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e956275285/gallecs-rural-area-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gallecs Rural Area Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31239559" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre Marimon – Sentmenat Castle loop from Mollet-Santa Rosa&lt;/a&gt;, providing convenient options for your ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Mollet Del Vallès?
A: Mollet Del Vallès offers good mountain biking conditions for much of the year. The region's climate allows for enjoyable riding in spring and autumn when temperatures are mild. Even in winter, many trails remain accessible, though some higher elevation or less exposed areas might be cooler or wetter. Summers can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon rides are often preferred.

Q: Are there specific areas known for their natural beauty or protected landscapes for mountain biking?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e956275285/gallecs-rural-area-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gallecs Special Conservation Area&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering an extensive rural protected area with numerous routes. Over half of Mollet del Vallès's area is green, largely due to Gallecs, providing exceptional natural surroundings for mountain biking and a serene escape.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain bike trails in Mollet Del Vallès?
A: While specific parking areas for every trail are not listed, Mollet Del Vallès and its surrounding natural areas, such as the Gallecs Special Conservation Area and access points to the Besòs River paths, typically have designated parking facilities or roadside parking available. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival or use navigation apps for specific starting points.

Q: Can I access mountain bike trails in Mollet Del Vallès using public transport?
A: Mollet Del Vallès is well-connected by public transport, making it feasible to reach trailheads. The city has train stations (Mollet-Sant Fost and Mollet-Santa Rosa) and bus services that can bring you close to various starting points, especially for routes within the Gallecs area or along the Besòs River. Consider checking local transport schedules for the most convenient access.
tips:
Place for the interpretation of the geological history of the formation of the Vallés-Penedés rift valley and the sedimentary materials that have filled it to this day, mostly Miocene. A mixture of sandstones, argillites and conglomerates can be visualized.

    It is also a space between fields with views of several farmhouses and the Can Veire forest.

The church of Santa María de Gallecs, erected in the 12th century, is a Romanesque temple that preserves some pre-Romanesque vestiges. It has a Latin cross plan and a rectangular apse, and originally the bell tower was an extension above the roof of one of the walls of the church and later became a tower.
   
   During the restoration works, Paleo-Christian remains from the 5th or 6th century were found. Located in a suggestive natural area, in its surroundings you can find some farmhouses of architectural interest.

Gallecs is a rural area just fifteen kilometers north of Barcelona. The main activity of the space is agriculture, and now the majority is organic farming. It is a very good space for leisure and sports. It is a green lung for the area!
Original and somewhat curious viewpoint in the middle of the Gallecs plains.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Espai Rural de Gallecs - circular mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: This circular mountain biking route is approximately 13.1 km long and generally takes about 1 hour and 9 minutes to complete. This duration can vary depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is the Espai Rural de Gallecs - circular trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is considered easy and is well-suited for beginners, families with children, and cyclists of all fitness levels. The terrain primarily consists of wide paths, making it very accessible.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is mostly wide paths that traverse agricultural fields and forests. You might encounter some paved sections as well. It's not technically demanding, making for a comfortable ride.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several points of interest, including the Gallecs Geological Section, the historic Santa Maria de Gallecs Church, and the expansive Gallecs Rural Area. There are also opportunities to enjoy a View of Gallecs Natural Park.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect while mountain biking here?
faqs.answer: Expect a picturesque rural landscape with traditional agricultural fields, small community gardens, and wooded areas. The scenery changes with the seasons, offering different colors in the fields. You might also spot indigenous fauna, especially migratory birds around wetlands like 'Els Aiguamolls de Can Salvi'.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike the Espai Rural de Gallecs - circular?
faqs.answer: The Espai Rural de Gallecs is enjoyable year-round, but spring and autumn offer particularly pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. The changing colors of the fields throughout the year add to the visual appeal, making it a great destination in any season.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Espai Rural de Gallecs - circular mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas for this exact route aren't detailed, the Espai Rural de Gallecs is generally well-equipped with parking facilities due to its popularity. Look for designated parking zones near the main access points to the rural area.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to reach the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Espai Rural de Gallecs is accessible by public transport, making it convenient for those without a car. Check local bus routes that serve the towns bordering the rural area, such as Parets del Vallès or Mollet del Vallès, which often have connections to Barcelona.

faqs.question: Is the Espai Rural de Gallecs - circular mountain biking route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Espai Rural de Gallecs is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural fields or near wildlife habitats, to ensure the safety and comfort of others and to protect the local environment.

faqs.question: Are there any permits, entrance fees, or special regulations for mountain biking in Espai Rural de Gallecs?
faqs.answer: As a protected natural space, there are no entrance fees or specific permits required for mountain biking in the Espai Rural de Gallecs. However, visitors are expected to respect the natural environment, stay on marked trails, and adhere to general park regulations to preserve this valuable area.

faqs.question: Does this mountain biking route intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route passes through sections of several other paths, including 'De la Tordera al Llobregat. Variant Palaudaries - Alella', 'Camí de Sant Valerià', 'Camí dels horts', and 'Camí dels Bandolers'.
